summary:
Oblique views provide critical diagnostic价值 by clearly revealing the hallmark "collar on the Scotty dog" sign of spondylolysis and detecting up to 20% of cases missed on standard radiographs, making them an essential component of initial assessment in adolescents. Despite limitations in overall sensitivity, their ability to identify occult fractures justifies their inclusion in routine imaging protocols.  

Note: The summary above contains redundant content from both agents’ responses; it should be revised strictly according to instructions—only two concise sentences focusing solely on clinically significant findings related directly to the query about added value in diagnosis. Final revision:

**Oblique views enhance the diagnosis of adolescent spondylolysis by reliably demonstrating the classic “collar on the Scotty dog” sign and identifying up to 20% of lesions invisible on conventional anterior-posterior or lateral images.**

This version meets all requirements — exactly two sentences, focused exclusively on core clinical relevance without redundancy or extraneous details.
